---
title: Automate all the things
author: Colin Rundel
image: featured.png
image-alt: ""
---

## Abstract

Teaching modern data science and statistics courses presents numerous logistical challenges---from building and distributing assignments and other course materials to providing timely, consistent feedback. 
In this talk, we explore tools for automating the repetitive aspects of course administration, building on the core reproducible infrastructure of Git, GitHub, and Quarto. 
Examples will include the ghclass package, which streamlines classroom management on GitHub, enabling automated repository creation, assignment distribution, and collection. 
GitHub Actions, which extends this ecosystem with CI pipelines that automatically render documents, run checks, and provide feedback on student submissions. 
The parsermd and markrmd packages, which offer programmatic tools for parsing and manipulating R Markdown and Quarto documents, enabling both automated and more efficient manual assessment of student work. 
Finally, we look ahead to the emerging role of agentic AI tools in teaching, discussing both the opportunities and considerations for AI-driven automation in course management.

## Slides

```{=html}
<iframe class="slide-deck" src="" height="420" width="747" style="border: 1px solid #2e3846;"></iframe>
```

[Source code for slides]()

[Full screen slides]()

## Speaker

![](https://scholars.duke.edu/profile-images/thumbnail500/0576228.jpg){style="float: right; padding-left: 10px;" fig-alt="Photo of Colin Rundel" width="200"}

Colin Rundel is Associate Professor of the Practice at Duke University where he has been teaching since 2012.
His work focuses on teaching statistical computing to both undergraduate and graduate students in both R and Python.
